# stalwart_account (Resource)

Manages an individual Stalwart account (the `Account` JMAP object with `@type` `User`).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "stalwart_account" "alice" {
  domain_id   = stalwart_domain.example.id
  name        = "alice"
  description = "Alice Example"
  password    = var.alice_password
  quota       = 5368709120 # 5 GiB in bytes
}

# Import an existing account by its email address:
# terraform import stalwart_account.alice alice@example.com
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Account name — the local part of the email address (the part before `@`). Changing it replaces the account.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Human-readable description of the account.
- `domain` (String) Name of the domain this object belongs to, e.g. `example.com`. Resolved to a domain id at apply time. Mutually exclusive with `domain_id`.
- `domain_id` (String) Opaque id of the domain this object belongs to. Mutually exclusive with `domain`.
- `member_of` (Set of String) Ids of groups this account is a member of (maps to `memberGroupIds`).
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password credential for the account. Write-only: the server never returns it, so it is not refreshed into state and out-of-band changes are not detected.
- `quota` (Number) Maximum disk space allocated to the account, in bytes (maps to the `maxDiskQuota` storage quota). Omit or set to 0 for unlimited.
- `role` (String) Built-in role for the account: `User`, `Admin`, or `Custom`. Defaults to `User`.
- `role_ids` (Set of String) Custom role ids assigned to the account, used when `role` is `Custom`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp of the account.
- `email_address` (String) Full email address of the account, formed as `name@domain` (server-set).
- `id` (String) Opaque server-assigned identifier of the account.
- `used_disk_quota` (Number) Disk space currently used by the account, in bytes (server-set).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by email address (preferred):
terraform import stalwart_account.alice alice@example.com

# Import by opaque server-assigned id:
# terraform import stalwart_account.alice <opaque-id>
```
